title: Comedy Gameplay YouTube Edit: Sizzle Cut, Reframed Shorts Variant, Meme Cutaway Graphics + Clickbait Thumbnail
grounding_note: VERBATIM from the brief: raw gameplay captures to be turned into an entertaining 1080p (or higher) YouTube upload encoded in H.264; comedy-sketch feel with rapid reaction zooms, text call-outs, meme-style cutaways and on-screen memes; aggressive trimming of dead air and jump cuts; client supplies the footage, rough time-stamped notes on funny moments, and custom graphics; deliverables = one fully edited 1080p H.264 video, a matching project file for later tweaks, and a thumbnail frame exported as a PNG. REASONABLY ENRICHED to make it a runnable long Adobe chain: (a) the meme cutaways / text call-outs are produced as concrete on-screen graphic assets — a clean-toned hero gameplay frame, two text-callout cards vectorized to SVG, and a duotone meme reaction tile — rather than left abstract; (b) a second vertical 1080x1920 reframed cut is added as a YouTube Shorts teaser since the connector exposes reframe; (c) all source media plus the produced graphics are organized into a CC project folder and a Firefly Board acts as the 'project file' reference board. NO capability is invented: no AI object removal, no generative fill (only solid fills + reframe-crop-expand are used), no text-to-image, no layout composition (the .indd is only EXPORTED from a client-authored title-card file). The hard creative judgement of WHERE jokes land is encoded as the client's time-stamped notes feeding the Quick Cut prompt, matching the brief's 'I'll supply my rough time-stamped notes'.
one_line_ask: Turn raw comedy gameplay footage plus client notes and custom graphics into a punchy 1080p H.264 YouTube cut with a vertical Shorts variant, polished meme/text-callout overlay graphics, and a clickbait thumbnail PNG — all organized into one reusable project package.
full_brief:
You are editing a comedy gameplay channel's next upload. The client hands you: a ~22-minute raw 1080p gameplay capture (ranked match, lots of dead air between highlights), a time-stamped notes sheet flagging the funny moments ('3:12 clutch fail', '7:48 teammate rage', '14:02 accidental teamkill', etc.), and a folder of custom graphics (a raw 'POV:' meme reaction screenshot and a hero gameplay still earmarked for the thumbnail).

Goal: a fully edited, fast-paced comedy YouTube piece that lands a laugh every few seconds — rapid reaction zooms, jump cuts, text call-outs, and meme-style cutaways — PLUS a matching reusable project package and a clickbait thumbnail.

Concretely:
1. Pull the client's footage, meme screenshot, hero still and notes into a managed Creative Cloud project folder.
2. Generate the on-screen comedy graphics from the supplied raw assets: straighten + auto-tone + crop the hero frame into a punchy 1280x720 thumbnail base; punch its contrast/saturation and apply a clickbait color overlay; turn the raw 'POV:' meme screenshot into a high-contrast duotone meme tile; and vectorize two meme/callout stills to clean SVGs so they stay crisp when zoomed.
3. Cut the gameplay down to a tight comedy edit driven by the client's funny-moment notes as free-text prompt steering, then produce a vertical Shorts teaser of the same energy.
4. Hand back: the edited 1080p H.264 master, the vertical Shorts cut, the thumbnail PNG, the overlay graphic set (duotone tile + 2 SVG callouts), and a project board/folder that ties every source + produced asset together so the client can tweak details later.

Stay within YouTube guidelines; humor tasteful; graphics crisp at zoom.

inputs.name: raw_gameplay_capture.mp4
inputs.gen_prompt: Screen-recorded first-person competitive shooter gameplay, 1080p, 22-minute-style ranked match feel rendered as a representative 40s clip: HUD with health/ammo/minimap, crosshair, kill-feed popups, occasional missed shots and a clumsy fall, long stretches of walking with little action between 2-3 highlight spikes. Flat game-capture look, slight compression, no cinematic grade.
inputs.realism_notes: Must look like genuine OBS/console screen-capture, not a cinematic render — flat exposure, real HUD elements, dead air between highlights so trimming has something to trim.

inputs.name: funny_moment_notes.csv
inputs.gen_prompt: CSV with columns timestamp,label,reaction_type for ~10 flagged comedy beats across a 22-min match, e.g. 00:03:12,clutch fail,zoom; 00:07:48,teammate rage,text-callout; 00:14:02,accidental teamkill,meme-cutaway; 00:19:30,victory dance,freeze-frame. Mix of zoom/text/meme/freeze reactions.
inputs.realism_notes: Reads like a creator's rough hand-typed notes — lowercase, terse labels, a couple of slightly inconsistent timestamp formats. Passed to Quick Cut only as free-text prompt steering, not as exact-timestamp trim points.

inputs.name: pov_meme_screenshot.png
inputs.gen_prompt: A meme-style reaction screenshot: bold white impact-font top text reading 'POV: your teammate has the aim of a potato', over a slightly blurry exaggerated in-game character close-up. Deliberately low-effort meme aesthetic with hard text outline.
inputs.realism_notes: Text-bearing meme image — use the text-capable model. Slight JPEG-ish softness so the duotone/halftone treatment has texture to bite into.

inputs.name: hero_thumbnail_frame.png
inputs.gen_prompt: A dramatic high-action gameplay still earmarked for a YouTube thumbnail: explosion/muzzle flash, character mid-dive, vivid orange-and-teal scene, slightly tilted handheld-capture framing, a little underexposed and crooked (so it visibly needs straighten + tone + crop). 1920x1080, photoreal game-render look.
inputs.realism_notes: Intentionally a few degrees off-level and slightly flat/underexposed so auto_straighten, auto_tone and the contrast/overlay steps have real work to do.

outputs.name: comedy_edit_master_1080p_h264.mp4
outputs.spec: Edited fast-paced comedy cut, 1920x1080, H.264/MP4, ~3-4 min target, produced by an AI Quick Cut driven by the funny-moment notes as free-text prompt steering — the YouTube master deliverable.

outputs.name: comedy_edit_shorts_1080x1920.mp4
outputs.spec: Vertical reframed teaser of the master cut, 1080x1920, H.264/MP4, SAME length as its source clip (reframe only, no trim) — YouTube Shorts variant.

outputs.name: youtube_thumbnail_1280x720.png
outputs.spec: Clickbait thumbnail: straightened, auto-toned, contrast/saturation-punched hero frame with clickbait color overlay, cropped/resized to 1280x720 PNG.

outputs.name: meme_cutaway_tile.png
outputs.spec: High-contrast duotone/halftone meme reaction tile derived from the 'POV:' screenshot, ready to drop in as an on-screen cutaway.

outputs.name: text_callout_card_1.svg
outputs.spec: Crisp whole-raster vectorization (SVG) of the duotone meme tile for on-screen call-outs, stays sharp under reaction zooms.

outputs.name: text_callout_card_2.svg
outputs.spec: Second whole-raster vectorization (SVG) of the styled hero frame, matching style, for a different beat.

outputs.name: project_package
outputs.spec: A CC project folder containing every source + produced asset plus a Firefly Board deep-link that ties them together as the reusable 'project file' reference.

connector_workflow.note: Create a managed Creative Cloud project folder ('Comedy_Gameplay_Edit') to hold all source + produced assets. Root of the deliverable package.
Initialize upload of the client-supplied source files (gameplay mp4, meme screenshot, hero still, notes csv) into project_folder.
Finalize the uploads, yielding stored CC asset handles for every source file — the chain's root inputs now live in the project.
Level the crooked hero gameplay still (the thumbnail base) — first step of the thumbnail chain.
Auto-tone the straightened hero frame to fix the underexposed/flat capture.
Punch vibrance/saturation on the toned hero frame for that high-energy clickbait pop.
Apply a warm clickbait color overlay (orange-teal push) on the punched hero frame for thumbnail vibe.
Crop + resize the overlaid hero frame to the 1280x720 YouTube thumbnail spec → thumbnail PNG deliverable.
Crank contrast on the raw 'POV:' meme screenshot to prep it for a punchy duotone treatment — start of the meme-tile chain.
Apply halftone/duotone to the contrasted meme screenshot → high-contrast meme cutaway tile deliverable.
Whole-raster vectorize the duotone meme tile into a clean SVG so it stays crisp under reaction zooms → SVG callout #1 deliverable. (Traces the whole image, not a text sub-region.)
Whole-raster vectorize the styled hero frame into a second clean SVG callout card (matching look, different beat) → SVG callout #2 deliverable. (Traces the whole image, not a sub-region.)

AI Quick Cut of the raw gameplay into a tight comedy sizzle; prompt driven by the client's funny-moment notes as free-text steering (NOT exact-timestamp trimming). 1080p H.264 master deliverable. Async-widget.
Reframe the master cut to vertical 1080x1920 (same length, no trim) for the YouTube Shorts teaser variant. Consumes the master from step 13. Async-widget.

connector_workflow.inputs_from: youtube_thumbnail_1280x720, meme_cutaway_tile, text_callout_card_1, text_callout_card_2, comedy_edit_master_1080p_h264, comedy_edit_shorts_1080x1920
connector_workflow.note: Assemble all produced asset URNs (thumbnail, meme tile, 2 SVG callouts, master + shorts cuts) into a Firefly Board deep-link as the reusable 'project file' reference board, alongside the project folder. Final package.

chaining_note: Strict output→input chain. Steps 1-3 stand up the project: create folder → init upload → finalize upload yields ingested_assets (every source file). The THUMBNAIL chain is fully linear: ingested hero still → auto_straighten(4) → auto_tone(5) → vibrance/sat(6) → color_overlay(7) → crop_and_resize(8)=thumbnail PNG. The MEME chain: ingested meme screenshot → brightness/contrast(9) → halftone(10)=meme tile → vectorize(11)=SVG callout #1 (whole-raster trace). SVG callout #2 (12) whole-raster vectorizes the styled hero frame from step 7. The VIDEO chain: Quick Cut(13) consumes the ingested gameplay, steered by the funny-moment notes as free text → master cut; video_resize(14) consumes that master → vertical Shorts (same length, reframe only). Finally create_firefly_board(15) consumes the outputs of 8,10,11,12,13,14 to assemble the reusable project package board. Every step after the initial asset load (step 3) consumes a prior step's named output — nothing is loaded twice from scratch.
difficulty_rationale: Long-horizon flagship: 15 connector calls, 14 distinct Adobe tools, spanning four connector groups (A4 asset/storage/board, A5 video, A1 photo/raster, A2 vector) with three parallel sub-chains (thumbnail, meme/callout graphics, video) that converge into one project-package deliverable — well past the ≥12 calls / ≥3 groups / ≥6 distinct tools flagship bar. Difficulty also comes from honest execution-mode mixing: the core video steps (13,14) are [A] async-widget (product-doable, not headless-retrievable), while the durable creative spine (thumbnail, meme tile, SVG callouts, folder, board) is all [C] headless-confirmed, so a grader can verify most of the deliverable autonomously. Faithful to a real Freelancer.com comedy-gameplay brief while staying strictly inside the capability sheet — no generative fill, no object removal, no text-to-image, no layout composition; image_fill_area avoided; video_resize used as same-length reframe only.

reverify.banned_capability_check: No banned capability appears in any step or in any prose field. No image_crop_and_resize crop-grow, no upscale/super-resolution, no text-to-image, no from-scratch art/logo, no object-removal-with-reconstruction. image_fill_area is not used. image_apply_lens_blur is not used. video_resize is used as a same-length vertical reframe only (no trim). image_crop_and_resize crops/reframes the hero frame (never grows it). image_vectorize traces whole rasters into SVGs. Quick Cut is prompt-driven from the client's funny-moment notes as free-text steering (no exact-timestamp trimming). Every input a step consumes is declared in inputs[].
reverify.notes: All 15 steps map to real Adobe connector tools and are gen-free. Task stays real, coherent, and fully connector-doable as a pure visual/video edit.
